name: cursor-remote
description: Remote control your local Cursor AI agent — monitor, approve, and send tasks from your phone or Telegram.

cursorRemote.autoStart.markdownDescription: Automatically start the relay server when Cursor launches.

cursorRemote.cdpUrl.markdownDescription: Cursor's Chrome DevTools Protocol endpoint URL. Launch Cursor with `--remote-debugging-port=9222` to enable. See the [setup guide](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote/blob/main/docs/setup-guide.md) for details.

cursorRemote.serverPort.markdownDescription: Port for the web client server.

cursorRemote.serverHost.markdownDescription: Bind address for the web client server. Default `127.0.0.1` only allows local access. Set to `0.0.0.0` for LAN/phone access (requires a password). For secure remote access, consider [Tailscale](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote/blob/main/docs/tailscale-setup.md).

cursorRemote.webappPassword.markdownDescription: Password to protect the web client. A random password is **auto-generated** on first install. See the [security section](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote#security) for best practices.

cursorRemote.windowTitleQualifier.markdownDescription: Include remote qualifier (e.g. WSL) in window titles.

cursorRemote.telegram.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable the Telegram bot transport. See the [Telegram setup guide](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote#telegram-setup) for step-by-step instructions.

cursorRemote.telegram.botToken.markdownDescription: Telegram bot token from [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather). See the [Telegram setup guide](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote#telegram-setup).

cursorRemote.telegram.allowedUsers.markdownDescription: Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to use the bot. See the [Telegram setup guide](https://github.com/len5ky/CursorRemote#telegram-setup).

cursorRemote.telegram.impl.markdownDescription: Telegram transport implementation. Use `grammy` (default) for the Grammy framework, or `raw` for a lightweight fetch-based transport if Grammy hangs on startup.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with CursorRemote
walkthroughs.description: Monitor sessions, approve steps, and send tasks from your phone or Telegram.

steps.title: Verify CDP Connection
steps.description:
Launch Cursor with `--remote-debugging-port=9222` and start the server.

[Start Server](command:cursorRemote.start)

steps.title: Set Up Network Access
steps.description:
By default, the server only listens on localhost. To access from your phone, open the Setup Panel.

[Open Setup Panel](command:cursorRemote.openSetup)

steps.title: Telegram Bot (optional)
steps.description:
Connect a Telegram bot for mobile notifications and commands.

[Set Up Telegram](command:cursorRemote.openSetup)

steps.title: Open Web Client
steps.description:
Everything is set up! Open the web client on your phone.

[Open Web Client](command:cursorRemote.openWebClient)

scripts.dev: tsx scripts/ensure-dirs.ts temp data && tsx scripts/dev-wrapper.ts
scripts.package: tsx scripts/ensure-dirs.ts releases && npx @vscode/vsce package --no-dependencies --out releases/ && tsx scripts/verify-vsix.ts
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build && npm run build:ext
scripts.start: tsx scripts/ensure-dirs.ts temp data && node dist/server/index.js
